title: Meeting a client at the airport

lines.target: David was waiting near the arrival lounge at Heathrow's Terminal One.
lines.base: David estaba esperando junto a la sala de llegadas de la terminal uno de Heathrow.

lines.target: He was waiting for a client who was coming from Geneva.
lines.base: Esperaba a un cliente que venía de Ginebra.

lines.target: The loudspeaker crackled and he heard a voice say: "British Airways announce the arrival of Flight One Oh Seven from Geneva".
lines.base: El altavoz crepitó y oyó una voz que decía: «British Airways anuncia la llegada del vuelo 107 procedente de Ginebra».

lines.target: David started looking for his client. He knew that the man would be wearing a carnation in his button-hole,
lines.base: David empezó a buscar a su cliente. Sabía que el hombre llevaría un clavel en el ojal,

lines.target: and carrying a copy of the "Sunday Times" under his arm.
lines.base: y llevaría un ejemplar del «Sunday Times» bajo el brazo.

lines.target: He caught sight of the man, who was tall and grey-haired with long sideboards.
lines.base: Vió al hombre, que era alto y de pelo gris, con largas patillas.

lines.target: He went forward and said: "You must be Mr Legarde? I'm David Wilson. How do you do?"
lines.base: Avanzó y dijo: «¿Es usted el Sr. Legarde? Soy David Wilson. ¿Cómo está usted?».

lines.target: They shook hands. David picked up the man's case and said:
lines.base: Se estrecharon las manos. David cogió la maleta del hombre y dijo:

lines.target: "Follow me, will you? My car is in the car park, it's just outside".
lines.base: «Sígame, ¿quiere? Mi coche está en el aparcamiento, ahí fuera».

lines.target: When they were driving towards London, David said: "I'll take you to your hotel first, then we'll get some lunch".
lines.base: Cuando iban hacia Londres dijo David: «Primero le llevaré a su hotel, luego tomaremos algo de comer».

lines.target: Mr Legarde replied: "Thank you. I'm staying at the Churchill. My secretary booked the room by telex".
lines.base: El Sr. Legarde respondió: «Gracias, estoy en el Churchill. Mi secretaria reservó la habitación por telex».

lines.target: At the hotel, the doorman took Mr Legarde's case and another servant parked the car.
lines.base: En el hotel, el portero cogió la maleta del Sr. Legarde y otro empleado aparcó el coche.

lines.target: The two men went to the reception desk: "My name's Legarde, I have a single room reserved".
lines.base: Los dos hombres fueron al mostrador de recepción: «Mi nombre es Legarde. Tengo reservada una habitación individual».

lines.target: "Yes sir, room two three seven . The hall-porter will show you up".
lines.base: «Sí señor, habitación 237. El conserje se la mostrará».

gap_candidates.target: David was waiting near the arrival lounge at Heathrow's Terminal One.
The loudspeaker crackled and he heard a voice say: "British Airways announce the arrival of Flight One Oh Seven (107) from Geneva".
David started looking for his client. He knew that the man would be wearing a carnation in his button-hole,
and carrying a copy of the "Sunday Times" under his arm.
He caught sight of the man, who was tall and grey-haired with long sideboards.
They shook hands. David picked up the man's case and said:
"Follow me, will you? My car is in the car park, it's just outside".
When they were driving towards London, David said: "I'll take you to your hotel first, then we'll get some lunch".
Mr Legarde replied: "Thank you. I'm staying at the Churchill. My secretary booked the room by telex".
At the hotel, the doorman took Mr Legarde's case and another servant parked the car.
The two men went to the reception desk: "My name's Legarde, I have a single room reserved".
"Yes sir, room two three seven (237). The hall-porter will show you up".
